title: fix(polly): render markdown formatting in table images and no postambles
author: fisventurous
pr_body_excerpt:
follow-up to #11194 - renders markdown formatting properly inside table images and possibly removes postamble questions

changes
\- strip `**bold**`, `*italic*`, `__underline__`, and backtick markers before drawing cell text onto table images
\- wire `_parse_text_formatting` into table rendering so bold/italic segments use the correct font variant (fonts were already loaded but never used)
\- measure column widths against stripped text for accurate sizing
\- add no-postamble voice rule to stop polly appending unsolicited follow-up questions like "want me to compare X vs Y?"
\- batch text + code blocks into one message when combined length fits under 2000 chars (fixes message fragmentation from #10639)

gist.headline: Pretty tables, fewer questions
gist.blurb: Polly's table images now properly render bold and italic text instead of showing raw markdown. We also taught her to stop asking unsolicited follow-up questions, making conversations much smoother.
gist.summary: Fixed markdown rendering in Polly's table images to properly display bold and italic fonts instead of raw markers, and added a no-postamble rule to prevent unsolicited follow-up questions.
gist.impact: Users will see cleaner, properly formatted text in generated table images and experience less message fragmentation, as text and code blocks under 2000 characters are now batched together.
gist.visual_concept: Polly the bee wearing a tiny painter's beret, carefully painting a neat, organized grid on a canvas and brushing away messy asterisk symbols.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of the cute bee mascot wearing a tiny painter's beret, carefully painting a neat, organized grid on a canvas. She is brushing away messy asterisk symbols to reveal clean text. Soft lime green (#ecf874) and lavender gradient background. Chunky pixels, warm lighting, lo-fi aesthetic like Stardew Valley. Emotionally warm, nostalgic but beautiful.
